item.arguments: Use $fw:brainstorm for a new internal audit trail feature that is still loosely defined.
special_constraints: The request is early and ambiguous.
success_definition: Focuses on requirements and scope.
Uses collaborative questions to deepen understanding.
Uses bounded options when the likely answers are predictable.
Right-sizes the brainstorming posture.
Preserves a clean handoff into /fw:plan.

item.arguments: Use $fw:brainstorm to figure out requirements for a product that helps engineers research unfamiliar technical topics with frontier models.
special_constraints: The request is early and ambiguous.
Proactively research on the user's behalf when it materially sharpens the requirements.
Still produce a requirements-oriented artifact, not a research report.
success_definition: Uses or explicitly invokes research rather than relying only on generic intuition.
Folds the decision-changing findings into requirements or the recommended direction.
Keeps the output centered on requirements, scope, and success criteria.
Mentions the durable brainstorm artifact and hands off into /fw:plan.

item.arguments: Use $fw:brainstorm for adding a small retry budget field to the worker admin UI.
special_constraints: Scope is small and already fairly clear.
success_definition: Stays requirements-focused without over-ceremony.
Still uses at least one targeted clarifying question when it would sharpen scope or success criteria.
Leaves a compact summary of what is understood versus open before planning.
Mentions the durable brainstorm artifact.
Hands off into planning.

item.arguments: Use $fw:brainstorm for a proposal to add a generalized orchestration layer before we have concrete consumers.
success_definition: Applies simplicity pressure.
Calls out carrying cost or speculative design risk.
Still keeps the work moving toward a clearer plan.

item.arguments: Use $fw:brainstorm for a change that may need a right-sized bounded context and ports/adapters boundary, and figure out the requirements before planning.
success_definition: Keeps the brainstorm focused on requirements and scope rather than implementation mechanics.
Captures high-level architecture direction only because it materially affects scope or behavior.
Still hands off into planning rather than turning brainstorm into a technical plan.
